title: Daily Plans and Particles
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct complete sentences using verbs and the wo/ni/de particles to describe their daily schedule.
instructions: Your friend is asking about your plans for today. Reply verbally in Japanese by translating the provided prompts. Pay close attention to using the correct particle for time (ni), destination (ni), and location of action (de).

contentItems.text: Speak clearly and confidently. Make sure your verbs end with a polite '~masu'.

speakingPrompt: Translate: 1) I go to school at 8 o'clock. 2) I read a book at the cafe. 3) I return home at 5 o'clock.
scenario: Walking to the train station with a friend, discussing your plans.

subTasks.prompt: Say: 'I go to school at 8 o'clock.'

sampleResponse.targetText: はちじに がっこうに いきます。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: I go to school at 8 o'clock.

subTasks.prompt: Say: 'I read a book at the cafe.'

sampleResponse.targetText: カフェで ほんを よみます。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: I read a book at the cafe.

sampleResponse.targetText: ごじに うちに かえります。

scenarioContext.targetText: きょうは なにを しますか。
scenarioContext.transliteration: Kyou wa nani wo shimasu ka.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: はちじに がっこうに いきます。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: I go to school at 8 o'clock.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: カフェで ほんを よみます。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: I read a book at the cafe.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: ごじに うちに かえります。

expectedSpeakingPoints: Say Hachi-ji ni gakkou ni ikimasu
Say Kafe de hon wo yomimasu
Say Go-ji ni uchi ni kaerimasu
acceptableParaphrases: Using 'Watashi wa' at the start is acceptable but not required.
Using 'Uchi e' instead of 'Uchi ni' is perfectly fine.

criteria.name: Particle Accuracy
criteria.description: Did the learner use 'de' for the cafe, 'wo' for the book, and 'ni' for times/destinations?

criteria.name: Verb Placement
criteria.description: Did the learner put the verbs at the end of the sentence?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: はちじに がっこうに いきます。カフェで ほんを よみます。ごじに うちに かえります。

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! You confidently built complex Japanese sentences with correct particles.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you use 'de' for reading AT the cafe, not 'ni'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to fully pronounce the 'wo' particle (as 'o') after the noun.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: きょうは なにを しますか。はちじに がっこうに いきます。カフェで ほんを よみます。ごじに うちに かえります。

hintText: Focus strictly on the particles. Time + ni. Destination + ni. Location + de. Object + wo.
